pattern: Once every element is tagged odd or even, the score of an index is just how many opposite-parity tags remain later in the array -- a single pass from the right, keeping a running count of each parity seen so far, answers every index in one shot.
hints: The score of index i only depends on how many odd or even numbers appear strictly after it -- nothing to the left ever matters.
Walk the array from right to left, keeping a running total of how many odd values and how many even values have been seen so far (not counting the current index).
At index i, if nums[i] is even, its score is the running odd-count; if nums[i] is odd, its score is the running even-count. Update the running counts after recording the answer.

edge_inputs.description: all even values, no opposite-parity partners anywhere
all odd values, no opposite-parity partners anywhere
opposite parity only at the very end
opposite parity only at the very start
all values at the maximum bound, alternating parity

stress.description: random values across the full length and value range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
